meta.title: SUDEP risk markers (Ochoa-Urrea 2025)
meta.authors: Ochoa-Urrea M, Luo X, Vilella L, et al.
meta.outcome: Five-year risk of sudden unexpected death in epilepsy (SUDEP) associated with individual risk markers — shown one marker at a time (this study did NOT publish a combined prediction model)
meta.cff_info:
What this shows. A prospective, multicentre cohort study (Ochoa-Urrea et al., Lancet 2025; 2632 patients, 8 centres) identified clinical and electroclinical risk markers for SUDEP (sudden unexpected death in epilepsy). For each marker it reports the observed 5-year SUDEP risk in patients who had it, versus those who did not, and versus the overall cohort (2.2%).

Important — this is not a calculator

  • The study did not build a combined risk model or score; it explicitly frames these markers as a basis for a future validatable SUDEP risk index. Each figure below is an independent (marginal) association — the risks cannot be added or multiplied together.
  • The strongest, routinely available markers are living alone (5-yr SUDEP 14.9% vs 2.1%; adjusted HR 8.25) and ≥3 generalised tonic–clonic seizures in the past year (4.3% vs 1.4%; HR 2.75).
  • The peri-ictal central apnoea markers require in-hospital video-EEG with cardiorespiratory monitoring and were not significant after full adjustment.

A reference to support SUDEP counselling and decisions about monitoring — not an individual risk prediction.

model.panel_title: 5-year SUDEP risk, one marker at a time
model.note: Read one marker at a time. These risks are NOT additive — a patient with two markers does not have their risks combined. Ochoa 2025 identified markers to guide monitoring; it did not publish a validated combined SUDEP score.
model.disclaimer: Independent (marginal) 5-year SUDEP risks from a single prospective cohort (Ochoa-Urrea et al., Lancet 2025). A risk-marker reference to support SUDEP counselling and monitoring — NOT a validated individual risk calculator. The peri-ictal apnoea markers require in-hospital cardiorespiratory monitoring and were not significant after full adjustment.
model.viz_info: Each bar is the 5-year SUDEP risk observed in patients who had that single marker (with its 95% CI whisker). The dashed red line is the overall cohort risk (2.2%). Bars are read one at a time and are never added together. Toggling a marker Yes just highlights it.

markers.name: Living alone
markers.info: Whether the patient lives alone (vs with someone). The strongest marker in this cohort — 5-year SUDEP risk 14.9% vs 2.1% (adjusted HR 8.25). Living alone reduces the chance that nocturnal convulsive seizures are witnessed and interrupted.

markers.name: ≥3 generalised tonic–clonic seizures in the past year
markers.info: Three or more generalised (focal-to-bilateral) tonic–clonic seizures in the previous year. 5-year SUDEP risk 4.3% vs 1.4% (adjusted HR 2.75). Results were similar using ‘one or more’ GCS or the raw seizure count.

markers.name: Prolonged postictal central apnoea (>14 s)
markers.info: Duration of central apnoea (no airflow, no respiratory effort) AFTER a seizure, measured during a recorded seizure with cardiorespiratory monitoring. Longer than the cohort median (14 s) was associated with 13.4% 5-year SUDEP risk unadjusted, but this was NOT statistically significant after adjusting for other factors. Not obtainable outside an epilepsy monitoring unit.

markers.name: Prolonged ictal central apnoea (>17 s)
markers.info: Duration of central apnoea DURING a seizure (video-EEG + respiratory monitoring). Longer than the cohort median (17 s) was associated with 7.4% vs 3.7%, but this did NOT reach statistical significance (p=0.11) and was not significant after adjustment. Not obtainable outside an epilepsy monitoring unit.

provenance.source: Ochoa-Urrea 2025 Lancet — Kaplan–Meier 5-year SUDEP probabilities per subgroup (Figure 2A–E + Results text) and adjusted HRs (Table 4). doi 10.1016/S0140-6736(25)01636-8
provenance.notes: NOT a prediction model — the paper reports risk markers only and explicitly leaves a combined 'validatable SUDEP risk index' to future work. Built as a transparent risk-MARKER reference (new 'markers' component kind): each marker shows its own marginal 5-yr SUDEP risk, never combined. Baseline overall 2.2% (1.3-3.0). Living alone 14.9%(4.1-24.4) vs 2.1%, adj HR 8.25; >=3 GCS/yr 4.3%(2.2-6.5) vs 1.4%, adj HR 2.75; postictal central apnoea >14s 13.4%(0-24.9) EMU, sig unadjusted only; ictal central apnoea >17s 7.4%(1.3-13.1) vs 3.7% EMU, non-significant (p=0.11). Built 2026-07-08.
